context: A caring, standard question to ask someone about their physical health or well-being.
A positive response indicating good health and normal energy levels.

front: Não estou me sentindo muito bem.
pronunciation: nohng ehs-TOW mee sehn-CHEEN-doo MOOI-too behng.
back: I am not feeling very well.
context: A polite way to announce that you are experiencing fatigue, discomfort, or mild illness.
Used to explicitly state that you are unwell. Female and male speakers use the same form.
The standard grammatical structure 'Estou com dor de...' means 'I am with pain of...'
A common, family-safe term used for abdominal discomfort or an upset stomach.
Refers to irritation or pain in the throat, often associated with a common cold.

back: I need to go to the doctor.
context: An essential phrase used when seeking professional medical attention or advice.
A crucial navigation question used to find emergency or inpatient medical centers.
Drugstores are highly ubiquitous in Brazil and often carry both medication and personal care items.
The standard action phrase for purchasing therapeutic remedies or prescriptions.
The official document from a doctor required to purchase controlled pharmaceuticals.
Used when your body temperature runs higher than normal due to fighting an infection.

back: I have the flu / a bad cold.
context: Refers to standard congestion or viral colds. Female speakers say 'gripada'.
A classic home remedy option commonly offered in Brazilian families to soothe mild illness.
Standard healthcare advice given universally to maintain hydration during recovery.
An encouraging statement made to a friend, colleague, or family member who looks tired or ill.
The collective biological structure comprising all limbs, organs, and systems.
Two primary upper body parts frequently cited in anatomical layouts or exercises.
The major limbs used for physical locomotion, lifting, moving, and exercising.
The extremeties of the limbs used for grasping objects and balanced walking/running.
The vital muscular organ responsible for pumping blood throughout the circulatory system.
The primary sensory organs dedicated to sight and hearing capabilities.
The primary facial features used for breathing, smelling, speaking, and eating.
A basic, essential daily dental hygiene routine practiced multiple times a day.

back: To wash the hands with soap
context: A crucial baseline hygiene practice to prevent the transmission of common germs.
The medical preventative act of receiving an immunization injection against diseases.
Brazilians widely use the brand name 'Band-Aid' colloquially to describe small skin bandages.
Refers to the private policy coverage used to pay for private medical clinic visits or tests.
The specific wing of a hospital open 24/7 for immediate, acute medical crises.
A warm, natural, and encouraging phrase wished to anyone recovering from an illness.
Said immediately when someone near you sneezes. Also used universally as a toast when drinking.

back: I am in good shape / fit.
context: Used to describe being in good physical condition through regular exercise and health habits.
Focusing your food choices on clean, nutrient-dense ingredients and balanced meals.
An extremely common cultural idiom used by Brazilians of all backgrounds to express relief regarding their health.
